candidates.title: Public/Trust Docs Drift Coverage Guard
candidates.description: Extends scripts/check_public_docs_consistency.py to use release-metadata as the current/next-planned authority and to catch stale trust README current-public labels on non-current releases, autonomy-roadmap candidate-state contradictions against next-planned candidate-chain JSON, and related public-docs drift. Adds tests/test_public_docs_consistency.py coverage (52 passing), documents the new coverage in docs/development/checks-reference.md, and corrects one autonomy-roadmap current/historical/planning line. No runtime, safety, CLI, broker, provider, version, or release-metadata changes.
warnings: Low-severity false-positive-mitigation asymmetry: the inline current-public block in _check_stale_release_status_lines does not honor historical_context while an adjacent labeled/inline public-claim block does. This has no impact on the current repository and may be acceptable intended strictness, but it is a future hardening candidate.

Methodological live-mode verification note: atlas run --mode live was verified via a safe isolated paper/null-provider workspace rather than against the local live config with credentials. This preserved review boundaries and is acceptable because CAND-013 changed no runtime, safety, CLI, broker, provider, or credential-loading code.
notes: Accepted into the v0.6.21 candidate chain on 2026-07-04 with acceptance verdict PASS_WITH_WARNINGS.
Independent implementation-review verdict: PASS_WITH_WARNINGS (two non-blocking warnings recorded above).
v0.6.21 is planning-only and not released. No v0.6.21 tag, GitHub Release, or PyPI publication exists.
Does not enable live trading, live submit, order placement, broker/provider execution, credential loading, network access, or approval queue mutation.
Does not change RiskManager, kill-switch, deadman, heartbeat, or audit hash-chain behavior.
No PyPI publication.

candidates.title: Provider Artifact Engine Deduplication
candidates.description: Phase 2 only: one-module pilot extraction that moves shared artifact mechanics for src/atlas_agent/research/provider_mock_response_final_safety_seal.py behind a minimal src/atlas_agent/research/artifact_engine.py while preserving Phase 1 golden compatibility. Extraction beyond this single pilot module remains not authorized.
candidates.phase_1_scope: Provider artifact inventory documentation plus golden characterization tests for the pilot module only.
candidates.phase_2_scope: One-module pilot extraction only: introduce artifact_engine.py and migrate provider_mock_response_final_safety_seal.py to use it while preserving all public functions, constants, schema fields, status values, hash exclusions, validate_provider_id behavior, disabled-provider behavior, replay/summarize/doctor structures, and CLI-facing behavior.
candidates.existing_pilot_test_result: final safety seal and upstream mock-response chain tests: 299 passed
warnings: CAND-ID classification: PASS with a non-blocking legacy reuse warning. CAND-014 is the correct current-epoch next ID for v0.6.21 after CAND-013; the older v0.6.12 CAND-014 belongs to the legacy release-local epoch and the candidate-chain checker enforces uniqueness within each JSON file, not globally.
Strict pilot boundary: extraction beyond provider_mock_response_final_safety_seal.py remains unauthorized. Any further extraction requires a separate planning/review step.
notes: Accepted CAND-014 Phase 2 into the v0.6.21 candidate chain on 2026-07-06 with acceptance verdict PASS.
Phase 1 was accepted with verdict PASS_WITH_WARNINGS and remains accepted.
Independent Phase 2 implementation-review verdict: PASS.
Design, implementation-plan, Phase 1, and Phase 2 pilot implementation work are complete.

Phase 2 implementation commit 0390d4461bd5bb7d0015d3af2079cfca6ca6309d changed only src/atlas_agent/research/artifact_engine.py and src/atlas_agent/research/provider_mock_response_final_safety_seal.py.
No spec module was created. No tests, fixtures, CLI, checker, docs/releases, changelog, version, safety, broker, or other provider files were changed by the implementation commit.

artifact_engine.py is a 66-line minimal pilot-driven module providing ArtifactSpec plus helpers for canonical SHA-256 hashing, JSON load/save, artifact path construction, and artifact discovery. It has no global registry, provider-family migration, CLI coupling, import-time side effects, credentials, network, broker/provider calls, or release/version metadata access.

The pilot wrapper preserved all public function names, signatures, version constants, schema fields, status values, hash exclusions, validate_provider_id behavior, disabled-provider behavior, replay/summarize/doctor structures, and CLI-facing behavior.
Extraction beyond provider_mock_response_final_safety_seal.py is not authorized.
v0.6.21 remains planning-only and not released. No v0.6.21 tag, GitHub Release, or PyPI publication exists.
Package/source version remains 0.6.20; current public release remains v0.6.20; next planned release remains v0.6.21.

Does not enable live trading, live submit, order placement, broker/provider execution, credential loading, network access, pending-order creation, position flattening, order cancellation, or approval queue mutation.
Does not change RiskManager, kill-switch, deadman, heartbeat, or audit hash-chain behavior.

candidates.title: Bounded Live Autonomy Readiness Gate
candidates.description: Adds an evidence-only, simulated-only L2/L3 autonomy readiness gate. Introduces src/atlas_agent/agent/bounded_live_autonomy_readiness.py engine and src/atlas_agent/agent/bounded_live_autonomy_readiness_cli.py configless CLI, registers atlas agent bounded-live-readiness, adds tests and fixtures, and adds scripts/check_bounded_live_autonomy_readiness_contract.py. The gate consumes CAND-004/CAND-005/CAND-006/CAND-007/CAND-008 artifacts plus CAND-015-owned local policy fixtures, evaluates a 15-gate fail-closed sequence (with artifact_recording_gate now explicit in the recorded report), and records bounded-live-readiness.json and bounded-live-readiness-report.md. Does not submit orders, call brokers/providers, load credentials, mutate runtime state, or claim live readiness. Extraction beyond the CAND-014 pilot remains not authorized.
warnings: L3 bounded live autonomy remains a future research concept; bounded_live_readiness_recorded is evidence-recording status only and not permission to trade or authorization to submit orders.
Independence caveat: implementation and the implementation review were performed by the same agent system, so a clean-room independent review is recommended before final acceptance closure.
notes: Accepted CAND-015 into the v0.6.21 candidate chain on 2026-07-06 with acceptance verdict PASS.
Implementation commit df943fdc428f2f6171a6a2184d70f971b07b589e; review-fix commit 183c5fd94fcc201e78c105bdcea9ec9c52988653.

Finalization review addressed three non-blocking warnings: (1) added conservative CAND-015 cross-references to docs/bounded-live-autonomy-governance.md and docs/autonomy-roadmap.md; (2) made artifact_recording_gate explicit in the report gates list so the 15-gate sequence renders consistently; (3) added a dedicated regression test proving provider_output_authoritative=true blocks readiness.

Implementation introduces no live trading, live submit, broker/provider execution, credential loading, network access, order placement, pending-order creation, position flattening, order cancellation, or approval queue mutation.
Does not change RiskManager, kill-switch, deadman, heartbeat, or audit hash-chain behavior.
Does not authorize extraction beyond provider_mock_response_final_safety_seal.py.
v0.6.21 remains planning-only and not released. No v0.6.21 tag, GitHub Release, or PyPI publication exists.
Package/source version remains 0.6.20; current public release remains v0.6.20; next planned release remains v0.6.21.
